1	/// JSON5 Token-Based Scanner/Parser
2	///
3	/// Parses JSON5 text into Expr AST values. JSON5 is a superset of JSON
4	/// based on ECMAScript 5.1 that supports comments, trailing commas,
5	/// unquoted keys, single-quoted strings, hex numbers, Infinity, NaN, etc.
6	///
7	/// Architecture: a scanner reads source bytes and produces typed tokens;
8	/// the parser only consumes tokens and never touches source/pos directly.
9	///
10	/// Reference: https://spec.json5.org/
11	pub const JSON5Parser = struct {
12	    source: []const u8,
13	    pos: usize,
14	    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
15	    stack_check: bun.StackCheck,
16	    token: Token,
17	
18	    const Token = struct {
19	        loc: logger.Loc,
20	        data: Data,
21	
22	        const Data = union(enum) {
23	            eof,
24	            // Structural
25	            left_brace,
26	            right_brace,
27	            left_bracket,
28	            right_bracket,
29	            colon,
30	            comma,
31	            // Values
32	            string: []u8,
33	            number: f64,
34	            boolean: bool,
35	            null,
36	            // Identifiers (for unquoted keys that aren't keywords)
37	            identifier: []u8,
38	
39	            fn canStartValue(data: Data) bool {
40	                return switch (data) {
41	                    .string, .number, .boolean, .identifier, .null, .left_brace, .left_bracket => true,
42	                    .eof, .right_brace, .right_bracket, .colon, .comma => false,
43	                };
44	            }
45	        };
46	    };
47	
48	    const ParseError = OOM || error{
49	        UnexpectedCharacter,
50	        UnexpectedToken,
51	        UnexpectedEof,
52	        UnterminatedString,
53	        UnterminatedComment,
54	        UnterminatedObject,
55	        UnterminatedArray,
56	        UnterminatedEscape,
57	        InvalidNumber,
58	        LeadingZeros,
59	        InvalidHexNumber,
60	        InvalidHexEscape,
61	        InvalidUnicodeEscape,
62	        OctalEscape,
63	        ExpectedColon,
64	        ExpectedComma,
65	        ExpectedClosingBrace,
66	        ExpectedClosingBracket,
67	        InvalidIdentifier,
68	        TrailingData,
69	        StackOverflow,
70	    };
71	
72	    pub const Error = union(enum) {
73	        oom,
74	        stack_overflow,
75	        unexpected_character: struct { pos: usize },
76	        unexpected_token: struct { pos: usize },
77	        unexpected_eof: struct { pos: usize },
78	        unterminated_string: struct { pos: usize },
79	        unterminated_comment: struct { pos: usize },
80	        unterminated_object: struct { pos: usize },
81	        unterminated_array: struct { pos: usize },
82	        unterminated_escape: struct { pos: usize },
83	        invalid_number: struct { pos: usize },
84	        leading_zeros: struct { pos: usize },
85	        invalid_hex_number: struct { pos: usize },
86	        invalid_hex_escape: struct { pos: usize },
87	        invalid_unicode_escape: struct { pos: usize },
88	        octal_escape: struct { pos: usize },
89	        expected_colon: struct { pos: usize },
90	        expected_comma: struct { pos: usize },
91	        expected_closing_brace: struct { pos: usize },
92	        expected_closing_bracket: struct { pos: usize },
93	        invalid_identifier: struct { pos: usize },
94	        trailing_data: struct { pos: usize },
95	
96	        pub fn addToLog(this: *const Error, source: *const logger.Source, log: *logger.Log) (OOM || error{StackOverflow})!void {
97	            const loc: logger.Loc = switch (this.*) {
98	                .oom => return error.OutOfMemory,
99	                .stack_overflow => return error.StackOverflow,
100	                inline else => |e| .{ .start = @intCast(e.pos) },

When the input is a string, this is a character offset. When the input is a `TypedArray`, this is a byte offset. Use `input.slice(read)` or `input.subarray(read)` to get the unconsumed remainder. */ 836 read: number; 837 /** `true` if all input was consumed successfully. `false` if the input ends with an incomplete value or a parse error occurred. */ 838 done: boolean; 839 /** A `SyntaxError` if a parse error occurred, otherwise `null`. Values parsed before the error are still available in `values`. */ 840 error: SyntaxError | null; 841 } 842 843 /** 844 * Parse a JSONL (JSON Lines) string into an array of JavaScript values. 845 * 846 * If a parse error occurs and no values were successfully parsed, throws 847 * a `SyntaxError`. If values were parsed before the error, returns the 848 * successfully parsed values without throwing. 849 * 850 * Incomplete trailing values (e.g. from a partial chunk) are silently 851 * ignored and not included in the result. 852 * 853 * When a `TypedArray` is passed, the bytes are parsed directly without 854 * copying if the content is ASCII. 855 * 856 * @param input The JSONL string or typed array to parse 857 * @returns An array of parsed values 858 * @throws {SyntaxError} If the input starts with invalid JSON and no values could be parsed 859 * 860 * @example 861 * ```js 862 * const items = Bun.JSONL.parse('{"a":1}\n{"b":2}\n'); 863 * // [{ a: 1 }, { b: 2 }] 864 * 865 * // From a Uint8Array (zero-copy for ASCII): 866 * const buf = new TextEncoder().encode('{"a":1}\n{"b":2}\n'); 867 * const items = Bun.JSONL.parse(buf); 868 * // [{ a: 1 }, { b: 2 }] 869 * 870 * // Partial results on error after valid values: 871 * const partial = Bun.JSONL.parse('{"a":1}\n{bad}\n'); 872 * // [{ a: 1 }] 873 * 874 * // Throws when no valid values precede the error: 875 * Bun.JSONL.parse('{bad}\n'); // throws SyntaxError 876 * ``` 877 */ 878 export function parse(input: string | NodeJS.TypedArray | DataView | ArrayBufferLike): unknown[]; 879 880 /** 881 * Parse a JSONL chunk, designed for streaming use. 882 * 883 * Never throws on parse errors. Instead, returns whatever values were 884 * successfully parsed along with an `error` property containing the 885 * `SyntaxError` (or `null` on success). Use `read` to determine how 886 * much input was consumed and `done` to check if all input was parsed. 887 * 888 * When a `TypedArray` is passed, the bytes are parsed directly without 889 * copying if the content is ASCII. Optional `start` and `end` parameters 890 * select a window of the input without copying. For typed arrays these 891 * are byte offsets and `read` will be a byte offset into the original 892 * typed array. For strings these are character offsets and `read` will 893 * be a character offset into the original string. 894 * 895 * @param input The JSONL string or typed array to parse 896 * @param start Offset to start parsing from (bytes for typed arrays, characters for strings, default: 0) 897 * @param end Offset to stop parsing at (bytes for typed arrays, characters for strings, default: input length) 898 * @returns An object with `values`, `read`, `done`, and `error` properties 899 * 900 * @example 901 * ```js 902 * let buffer = new Uint8Array(0); 903 * for await (const chunk of stream) { 904 * buffer = Buffer.concat([buffer, chunk]); 905 * const { values, read, error } = Bun.JSONL.parseChunk(buffer); 906 * if (error) throw error; 907 * for (const value of values) handle(value); 908 * buffer = buffer.subarray(read); 909 * } 910 * ``` 911 */ 912 export function parseChunk( 913 input: string | NodeJS.TypedArray | DataView | ArrayBufferLike, 914 start?: number, 915 end?: number, 916 ): ParseChunkResult; 917 } 918 919 /** 920 * YAML related APIs 921 */ 922 namespace YAML { 923 /** 924 * Parse a YAML string into a JavaScript value 925 * 926 * @category Utilities 927 * 928 * @param input The YAML string to parse 929 * @returns A JavaScript value 930 * 931 * @example 932 * ```ts 933 * import { YAML } from "bun"; 934 * 935 * console.log(YAML.parse("123")) // 123 936 * console.log(YAML.parse("null")) // null 937 * console.log(YAML.parse("false")) // false 938 * console.log(YAML.parse("abc")) // "abc" 939 * console.log(YAML.parse("- abc")) // [ "abc" ] 940 * console.log(YAML.parse("abc: def")) // { "abc": "def" } 941 * ``` 942 */ 943 export function parse(input: string): unknown; 944 945 /** 946 * Convert a JavaScript value into a YAML string. Strings are double quoted if they contain keywords, non-printable or 947 * escaped characters, or if a YAML parser would parse them as numbers. Anchors and aliases are inferred from objects, allowing cycles. 948 * 949 * @category Utilities 950 * 951 * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify. 952 * @param replacer Currently not supported. 953 * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation. 954 * Without this parameter, outputs flow-style (single-line) YAML. 955 * With this parameter, outputs block-style (multi-line) YAML. 956 * The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used. 957 * @returns A string containing the YAML document. 958 * 959 * @example 960 * ```ts 961 * import { YAML } from "bun"; 962 * 963 * const input = { 964 * abc: "def", 965 * num: 123 966 * }; 967 * 968 * // Without space - flow style (single-line) 969 * console.log(YAML.stringify(input)); 970 * // {abc: def,num: 123} 971 * 972 * // With space - block style (multi-line) 973 * console.log(YAML.stringify(input, null, 2)); 974 * // abc: def 975 * // num: 123 976 * 977 * const cycle = {}; 978 * cycle.obj = cycle; 979 * console.log(YAML.stringify(cycle, null, 2)); 980 * // &1 981 * // obj: *1 982 */ 983 export function stringify(input: unknown, replacer?: undefined | null, space?: string | number): string; 984 } 985 986 /** 987 * Markdown related APIs. 988 * 989 * Provides fast markdown parsing and rendering with three output modes: 990 * - `html()` — render to an HTML string 991 * - `render()` — render with custom callbacks for each element 992 * - `react()` — parse to React-compatible JSX elements 993 * 994 * Supports GFM extensions (tables, strikethrough, task lists, autolinks) and 995 * component overrides to replace default HTML tags with custom components. 996 * 997 * @example 998 * ```tsx 999 * // Render markdown to HTML 1000 * const html = Bun.markdown.html("# Hello **world**"); 1001 * // "

28	            right_bracket,

33	            number: f64,
34	            boolean: bool,
35	            null,
36	            // Identifiers (for unquoted keys that aren't keywords)
37	            identifier: []u8,

41	                    .string, .number, .boolean, .identifier, .null, .left_brace, .left_bracket => true,
42	                    .eof, .right_brace, .right_bracket, .colon, .comma => false,
43	                };
44	            }

The stringify function should reverse this process, putting flat keys in the global section and nested objects as sections.

Since INI is much simpler than TOML or YAML, I can directly output an Expr object rather than building a full AST, which keeps things consistent with the codebase while staying straightforward. The format itself is pretty minimal—comments with `;` or `#`, section headers in brackets, and simple key-value pairs with optional spacing around the equals sign.

For the stringify side, I need to handle root-level primitives as plain `key = value` lines, convert nested objects into `[section]` blocks with their properties indented underneath, and decide how to handle anything deeper than two levels—probably throw an error since INI doesn't really support that. I'm settling on using the Expr infrastructure for consistency with the other parsers, even though INI is simple enough to build the object directly. The parser will create an Expr tree by iterating through lines, handling comments, section headers, and key-value pairs, then converting to JS values. For stringify, I'll work directly with the JS object structure, outputting primitives as key-value pairs and objects as section headers.

input.content:
# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as public namespaces
for parsing/stringifying structured text formats. Each follows the same 4-layer pattern:

```
bun.d.ts (TS types)
  → BunObject.cpp "bunObjectTable" + BunObject+exports.h
    → BunObject.zig lazy getter + exportAll()
      → api.zig re-export
        → src/bun.js/api/XxxObject.zig  (parse / stringify)
          → src/interchange/xxx.zig      (actual parser → returns Expr AST)
```

The goal is to wire `Bun.INI` through all of these layers so it works end-to-end without
importing the npm `ini` package. The parser will be self-contained in Zig.

---

## INI Grammar (subset supported)

```
; comment
# comment

key = value           ; root-level key/value

[section]
key = value           ; nested under section object

; inline comments on values: key = value ; ignored
```

Rules:
- Lines trimmed of surrounding whitespace before parsing
- Lines starting with `;` or `#` → skip
- Empty lines → skip
- `[section]` → next keys go into a nested object
- `key = value` → string key, string value (trimmed); inline `;`/`#` comments stripped
- Parse result: nested plain JS object; all values are strings

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/interchange/ini.zig`

Implements `pub const INI.parse(source, log, allocator) !Expr`.

Uses `std.mem.splitAny(u8, source.contents, "\n")` to iterate lines. For each line:
- Trim whitespace
- Skip empty / comment lines (`[0] == ';'` or `'#'`)
- `[section]` → extract name, create `Expr.init(E.Object, E.Object{}, loc)`, push as
  property of root, set `current_section`
- `key = value` → find first `=`, trim both sides, strip inline `;`/`#` from value,
  create `E.String` exprs for both, push `G.Property{.key=…, .value=…}` onto the target
  object's `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)`

Property building mirrors the json5 parser pattern exactly:

```zig
var props = std.array_list.Managed(G.Property).init(allocator);
try props.append(.{ .key = key_expr, .value = val_expr });
const obj_expr = Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = .moveFromList(&props) }, loc);
```

**Imports at bottom** (Zig convention):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const E = ast.E;
const G = ast.G;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
```

### 2. `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`

**`pub fn create(globalThis) JSValue`** – creates empty object with 2 slots, puts `parse`
(1 arg) and `stringify` (1 arg, no replacer/space since INI is flat).

**`pub fn parse(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – mirrors JSON5Object.zig exactly:
- `ArenaAllocator` + `ASTMemoryAllocator` scope
- `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input
- `bun.interchange.ini.INI.parse(source, &log, allocator)`
- Error: `global.createSyntaxErrorInstance("INI Parse error: {s}", ...)`
- Success: call `exprToJS(root, global)` (same helper as JSON5Object)

**`fn exprToJS(expr, global) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – identical to JSON5Object version,
handles `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, `e_object`. Since INI
only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

**Imports at bottom** (same pattern as JSON5Object.zig / YAMLObject.zig):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
const jsc = bun.jsc;
const JSValue = jsc.JSValue;
const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

### 3. `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`

Tests using `bun:test`. Examples:
```ts
import { expect, test, describe } from "bun:test";

describe("Bun.INI.parse", () => {
  test("parses key=value pairs", ...);
  test("parses sections", ...);
  test("skips ; and # comments", ...);
  test("strips inline comments from values", ...);
  test("handles empty input", ...);
  test("throws on missing =", ...);
});

describe("Bun.INI.stringify", () => {
  test("stringifies flat objects", ...);
  test("stringifies objects with sections", ...);
  test("returns undefined for non-object input", ...);
});
```

---

## Files to Modify

### 4. `src/interchange.zig`

**Add** after line 4 (after `yaml`):
```zig
pub const ini = @import("./interchange/ini.zig");
```

### 5. `src/bun.js/api.zig`

**Add** after line 34 (after `YAMLObject`):
```zig
pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");
```

### 6. `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig`

Three changes:

**a) In `BunObject` struct** (after line 70, after `YAML`):
```zig
pub const INI = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getINIObject);
```

**b) In `exportAll()`** (after line 140, after `YAML` export):
```zig
@export(&BunObject.INI, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("INI") });
```

**c) Getter function** (after `getYAMLObject` at ~line 1289):
```zig
pub fn getINIObject(globalThis: *jsc.JSGlobalObject, _: *jsc.JSObject) jsc.JSValue {
    return INIObject.create(globalThis);
}
```

**d) Import at bottom** (after line 2083, after `YAMLObject`):
```zig
const INIObject = bun.api.INIObject;
```

### 7. `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h`

**Add** `macro(INI)` in the `FOR_EACH_GETTER` list (alphabetically between `Glob` and `JSON5`,
i.e., after line 11):
```c
macro(INI) \
```

### 8. `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`

**Add** in `@begin bunObjectTable` section (alphabetically near other format namespaces, 
after the `JSONL` line ~965):
```
INI                                            BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI                                       DontDelete|PropertyCallback
```

### 9. `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`

**Add** after the `JSONC` namespace (after line 821):
```ts
/**
 * INI related APIs
 */
namespace INI {
  /**
   * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.
   *
   * Supports sections (as nested objects), key=value pairs, and
   * comment lines starting with `;` or `#`. Inline comments are
   * also stripped from values.
   *
   * @category Utilities
   *
   * @param input The INI string to parse
   * @returns A JavaScript object
   *
   * @example
   * ```ts
   * import { INI } from "bun";
   *
   * const result = INI.parse(`
   *   ; comment
   *   name = Alice
   *
   *   [database]
   *   host = localhost
   *   port = 5432
   * `);
   * // { name: "Alice", database: { host: "localhost", port: "5432" } }
   * ```
   */
  export function parse(input: string): Record<string, string | Record<string, string>>;

  /**
   * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI string.
   *
   * Root-level scalar properties become flat `key = value` lines.
   * Root-level object properties become `[section]` blocks.
   * Arrays and values nested more than one level deep are not supported.
   *
   * @category Utilities
   *
   * @param input The JavaScript object to stringify
   * @returns A string containing the INI document
   *
   * @example
   * ```ts
   * import { INI } from "bun";
   *
   * const result = INI.stringify({
   *   name: "Alice",
   *   database: { host: "localhost", port: "5432" },
   * });
   * // name = Alice
   * // [database]
   * // host = localhost
   * // port = 5432
   * ```
   */
  export function stringify(input: Record<string, unknown>): string;
}
```

---

## Critical File Paths

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/interchange/ini.zig` | **CREATE** – pure Zig INI parser |
| `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` | **CREATE** – JS binding (parse + stringify) |
| `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts` | **CREATE** – test suite |
| `src/interchange.zig` | **MODIFY** – add `pub const ini` |
| `src/bun.js/api.zig` | **MODIFY** – add `pub const INIObject` |
| `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | **MODIFY** – 4 additions |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` | **MODIFY** – add `macro(INI)` |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` | **MODIFY** – add table entry |
| `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts` | **MODIFY** – add `namespace INI` |

---

## Existing Utilities to Reuse

| Utility | Location |
|---------|----------|
| `bun.ArenaAllocator` | Pattern from YAMLObject/JSON5Object |
| `ast.ASTMemoryAllocator` | Same arena setup as JSON5Object |
| `jsc.Node.BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` | Flexible input (strings, buffers) |
| `logger.Log` + `logger.Source.initPathString` | Consistent error logging |
| `exprToJS(Expr, global) JSValue` | Copy from JSON5Object.zig |
| `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)` + `.moveFromList` | JSON5 parser AST building pattern |
| `jsc.JSPropertyIterator` | Used in YAMLObject stringify |
| `wtf.StringBuilder` + `.toString(global)` | Used in YAML/JSON5 stringify |
| `Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = … }, loc)` | JSON5 parser pattern |
| `Expr.init(E.String, E.String{ .data = … }, loc)` | JSON5 parser pattern |

---

## Verification

After implementation (do NOT build during planning):

```bash
# Run the test file
bun bd test test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts

# Quick smoke test
bun bd -e "console.log(Bun.INI.parse('[s]\nk=v'))"
# Expected: { s: { k: 'v' } }

bun bd -e "console.log(Bun.INI.stringify({ name: 'Alice', db: { host: 'localhost' } }))"
# Expected: name = Alice\n[db]\nhost = localhost\n
```

Also verify TypeScript types are correct with:
```bash
cd packages/bun-types && bun run build 2>&1 | head -20
```

only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

**Imports at bottom** (same pattern as JSON5Object.zig / YAMLObject.zig):

const jsc = bun.jsc;
const JSValue = jsc.JSValue;
const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as public namespaces
for parsing/stringifying structured text formats. Each follows the same 4-layer pattern:

```
bun.d.ts (TS types)
  → BunObject.cpp "bunObjectTable" + BunObject+exports.h
    → BunObject.zig lazy getter + exportAll()
      → api.zig re-export
        → src/bun.js/api/XxxObject.zig  (parse / stringify)
          → src/interchange/xxx.zig      (actual parser → returns Expr AST)
```

The goal is to wire `Bun.INI` through all of these layers so it works end-to-end without
importing the npm `ini` package. The parser will be self-contained in Zig.

---

## INI Grammar (subset supported)

```
; comment
# comment

key = value           ; root-level key/value

[section]
key = value           ; nested under section object

; inline comments on values: key = value ; ignored
```

Rules:
- Lines trimmed of surrounding whitespace before parsing
- Lines starting with `;` or `#` → skip
- Empty lines → skip
- `[section]` → next keys go into a nested object
- `key = value` → string key, string value (trimmed); inline `;`/`#` comments stripped
- Parse result: nested plain JS object; all values are strings

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/interchange/ini.zig`

Implements `pub const INI.parse(source, log, allocator) !Expr`.

Uses `std.mem.splitAny(u8, source.contents, "\n")` to iterate lines. For each line:
- Trim whitespace
- Skip empty / comment lines (`[0] == ';'` or `'#'`)
- `[section]` → extract name, create `Expr.init(E.Object, E.Object{}, loc)`, push as
  property of root, set `current_section`
- `key = value` → find first `=`, trim both sides, strip inline `;`/`#` from value,
  create `E.String` exprs for both, push `G.Property{.key=…, .value=…}` onto the target
  object's `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)`

Property building mirrors the json5 parser pattern exactly:

```zig
var props = std.array_list.Managed(G.Property).init(allocator);
try props.append(.{ .key = key_expr, .value = val_expr });
const obj_expr = Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = .moveFromList(&props) }, loc);
```

**Imports at bottom** (Zig convention):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const E = ast.E;
const G = ast.G;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
```

### 2. `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`

**`pub fn create(globalThis) JSValue`** – creates empty object with 2 slots, puts `parse`
(1 arg) and `stringify` (1 arg, no replacer/space since INI is flat).

**`pub fn parse(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – mirrors JSON5Object.zig exactly:
- `ArenaAllocator` + `ASTMemoryAllocator` scope
- `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input
- `bun.interchange.ini.INI.parse(source, &log, allocator)`
- Error: `global.createSyntaxErrorInstance("INI Parse error: {s}", ...)`
- Success: call `exprToJS(root, global)` (same helper as JSON5Object)

**`fn exprToJS(expr, global) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – identical to JSON5Object version,
handles `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, `e_object`. Since INI
only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

**Imports at bottom** (same pattern as JSON5Object.zig / YAMLObject.zig):
```zig

const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
const jsc = bun.jsc;
const JSValue = jsc.JSValue;
const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

### 3. `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`

Tests using `bun:test`. Examples:
```ts

  test("parses key=value pairs", ...);
  test("parses sections", ...);

});

```

### 2. `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`

**`pub fn create(globalThis) JSValue`** – creates empty object with 2 slots, puts `parse`
(1 arg) and `stringify` (1 arg, no replacer/space since INI is flat).

**`pub fn parse(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – mirrors JSON5Object.zig exactly:
- `ArenaAllocator` + `ASTMemoryAllocator` scope
- `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input
- `bun.interchange.ini.INI.parse(source, &log, allocator)`
- Error: `global.createSyntaxErrorInstance("INI Parse error: {s}", ...)`
- Success: call `exprToJS(root, global)` (same helper as JSON5Object)

**`fn exprToJS(expr, global) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – identical to JSON5Object version,
handles `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, `e_object`. Since INI
only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

```zig

const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

namespace INI {
  /**
   * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.

   * Supports sections (as nested objects), key=value pairs, and

   *   port = 5432

   * ```

  export function parse(input: string): Record<string, string | Record<string, string>>;

   * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI string.

   * Root-level object properties become `[section]` blocks.

   * // [database]
   * // host = localhost
   * // port = 5432
   * ```

  export function stringify(input: Record<string, unknown>): string;
}

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as public namespaces
for parsing/stringifying structured text formats. Each follows the same 4-layer pattern:

```
bun.d.ts (TS types)
  → BunObject.cpp "bunObjectTable" + BunObject+exports.h
    → BunObject.zig lazy getter + exportAll()
      → api.zig re-export
        → src/bun.js/api/XxxObject.zig  (parse / stringify)
          → src/interchange/xxx.zig      (actual parser → returns Expr AST)
```

The goal is to wire `Bun.INI` through all of these layers so it works end-to-end without
importing the npm `ini` package. The parser will be self-contained in Zig.

---

## INI Grammar (subset supported)

```
; comment
# comment

key = value           ; root-level key/value

[section]
key = value           ; nested under section object

; inline comments on values: key = value ; ignored
```

Rules:
- Lines trimmed of surrounding whitespace before parsing
- Lines starting with `;` or `#` → skip
- Empty lines → skip
- `[section]` → next keys go into a nested object
- `key = value` → string key, string value (trimmed); inline `;`/`#` comments stripped
- Parse result: nested plain JS object; all values are strings

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/interchange/ini.zig`

Implements `pub const INI.parse(source, log, allocator) !Expr`.

Uses `std.mem.splitAny(u8, source.contents, "\n")` to iterate lines. For each line:
- Trim whitespace
- Skip empty / comment lines (`[0] == ';'` or `'#'`)
- `[section]` → extract name, create `Expr.init(E.Object, E.Object{}, loc)`, push as
  property of root, set `current_section`
- `key = value` → find first `=`, trim both sides, strip inline `;`/`#` from value,
  create `E.String` exprs for both, push `G.Property{.key=…, .value=…}` onto the target
  object's `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)`

Property building mirrors the json5 parser pattern exactly:

```zig
var props = std.array_list.Managed(G.Property).init(allocator);
try props.append(.{ .key = key_expr, .value = val_expr });
const obj_expr = Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = .moveFromList(&props) }, loc);
```

**Imports at bottom** (Zig convention):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const E = ast.E;
const G = ast.G;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
```

### 2. `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`

**`pub fn create(globalThis) JSValue`** – creates empty object with 2 slots, puts `parse`
(1 arg) and `stringify` (1 arg, no replacer/space since INI is flat).

**`pub fn parse(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – mirrors JSON5Object.zig exactly:
- `ArenaAllocator` + `ASTMemoryAllocator` scope
- `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input
- `bun.interchange.ini.INI.parse(source, &log, allocator)`
- Error: `global.createSyntaxErrorInstance("INI Parse error: {s}", ...)`
- Success: call `exprToJS(root, global)` (same helper as JSON5Object)

**`fn exprToJS(expr, global) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – identical to JSON5Object version,
handles `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, `e_object`. Since INI
only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

**Imports at bottom** (same pattern as JSON5Object.zig / YAMLObject.zig):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
const jsc = bun.jsc;
const JSValue = jsc.JSValue;
const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

### 3. `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`

Tests using `bun:test`. Examples:
```ts

describe("Bun.INI.parse", () => {
  test("parses key=value pairs", ...);
  test("parses sections", ...);
  test("skips ; and # comments", ...);
  test("strips inline comments from values", ...);
  test("handles empty input", ...);
  test("throws on missing =", ...);
});

describe("Bun.INI.stringify", () => {

  test("stringifies objects with sections", ...);

```

---

```

### 8. `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`

**Add** in `@begin bunObjectTable` section (alphabetically near other format namespaces,

```

### 9. `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`

**Add** after the `JSONC` namespace (after line 821):
```ts

2. **`src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`** — JS API binding. Exposes `parse` (string → JS object) and `stringify` (JS object → INI string). Follows the JSON5Object.zig pattern exactly. Stringify emits root-level scalars as flat `key = value`, then object properties as `[section]` blocks.
3. **`test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`** — Tests for parse (sections, comments, inline comments, empty input, error cases) and stringify (flat objects, objects with sections, edge cases).

### Files to **modify** (6 existing files):
4. **`src/interchange.zig`** — Add `pub const ini = @import("./interchange/ini.zig");`
5. **`src/bun.js/api.zig`** — Add `pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");`
6. **`src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig`** — 4 additions: lazy property declaration, `exportAll()` registration, `getINIObject()` getter function, and bottom import
7. **`src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h`** — Add `macro(INI)` to `FOR_EACH_GETTER`
8. **`src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`** — Add `INI BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI DontDelete|PropertyCallback` to the table
9. **`packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`** — Add `namespace INI` with full JSDoc for `parse` and `stringify`

The implementation reuses existing infrastructure: `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input, `jsc.JSPropertyIterator` for stringify, `wtf.StringBuilder` for output, and the exact `exprToJS` helper and `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)` + `.moveFromList` patterns from JSON5Object/JSON5 parser.
tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `Bun.INI` Namespace

## Context

Bun already exposes `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML` as public namespaces
for parsing/stringifying structured text formats. Each follows the same 4-layer pattern:

```
bun.d.ts (TS types)
  → BunObject.cpp "bunObjectTable" + BunObject+exports.h
    → BunObject.zig lazy getter + exportAll()
      → api.zig re-export
        → src/bun.js/api/XxxObject.zig  (parse / stringify)
          → src/interchange/xxx.zig      (actual parser → returns Expr AST)
```

The goal is to wire `Bun.INI` through all of these layers so it works end-to-end without
importing the npm `ini` package. The parser will be self-contained in Zig.

---

## INI Grammar (subset supported)

```
; comment
# comment

key = value           ; root-level key/value

[section]
key = value           ; nested under section object

; inline comments on values: key = value ; ignored
```

Rules:
- Lines trimmed of surrounding whitespace before parsing
- Lines starting with `;` or `#` → skip
- Empty lines → skip
- `[section]` → next keys go into a nested object
- `key = value` → string key, string value (trimmed); inline `;`/`#` comments stripped
- Parse result: nested plain JS object; all values are strings

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/interchange/ini.zig`

Implements `pub const INI.parse(source, log, allocator) !Expr`.

Uses `std.mem.splitAny(u8, source.contents, "\n")` to iterate lines. For each line:
- Trim whitespace
- Skip empty / comment lines (`[0] == ';'` or `'#'`)
- `[section]` → extract name, create `Expr.init(E.Object, E.Object{}, loc)`, push as
  property of root, set `current_section`
- `key = value` → find first `=`, trim both sides, strip inline `;`/`#` from value,
  create `E.String` exprs for both, push `G.Property{.key=…, .value=…}` onto the target
  object's `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)`

Property building mirrors the json5 parser pattern exactly:

```zig
var props = std.array_list.Managed(G.Property).init(allocator);
try props.append(.{ .key = key_expr, .value = val_expr });
const obj_expr = Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = .moveFromList(&props) }, loc);
```

**Imports at bottom** (Zig convention):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const E = ast.E;
const G = ast.G;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
```

### 2. `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`

**`pub fn create(globalThis) JSValue`** – creates empty object with 2 slots, puts `parse`
(1 arg) and `stringify` (1 arg, no replacer/space since INI is flat).

**`pub fn parse(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – mirrors JSON5Object.zig exactly:
- `ArenaAllocator` + `ASTMemoryAllocator` scope
- `BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` for flexible input
- `bun.interchange.ini.INI.parse(source, &log, allocator)`
- Error: `global.createSyntaxErrorInstance("INI Parse error: {s}", ...)`
- Success: call `exprToJS(root, global)` (same helper as JSON5Object)

**`fn exprToJS(expr, global) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – identical to JSON5Object version,
handles `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, `e_object`. Since INI
only produces strings and objects, the other cases gracefully return `.js_undefined`.

**`pub fn stringify(global, callframe) bun.JSError!JSValue`** – takes 1 argument (the value):
- Reject non-objects / non-serializable types with `.js_undefined`
- Iterate root properties via `jsc.JSPropertyIterator`
- For scalar properties (string, number, boolean): emit `key = value\n`
- For object sub-properties: emit `[section]\n` then iterate inner object for `key = value\n`
- Reject nested objects deeper than 1 level (throw `"INI.stringify: nested objects beyond one level are not supported"`)
- Reject arrays (skip silently or throw, documented in types)
- Use `wtf.StringBuilder` + `builder.toString(global)` for output

**Imports at bottom** (same pattern as JSON5Object.zig / YAMLObject.zig):
```zig
const std = @import("std");
const bun = @import("bun");
const logger = bun.logger;
const ast = bun.ast;
const Expr = ast.Expr;
const jsc = bun.jsc;
const JSValue = jsc.JSValue;
const ZigString = jsc.ZigString;
const wtf = jsc.wtf;
```

### 3. `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`

Tests using `bun:test`. Examples:
```ts
import { expect, test, describe } from "bun:test";

describe("Bun.INI.parse", () => {
  test("parses key=value pairs", ...);
  test("parses sections", ...);
  test("skips ; and # comments", ...);
  test("strips inline comments from values", ...);
  test("handles empty input", ...);
  test("throws on missing =", ...);
});

describe("Bun.INI.stringify", () => {
  test("stringifies flat objects", ...);
  test("stringifies objects with sections", ...);
  test("returns undefined for non-object input", ...);
});
```

---

## Files to Modify

### 4. `src/interchange.zig`

**Add** after line 4 (after `yaml`):
```zig
pub const ini = @import("./interchange/ini.zig");
```

### 5. `src/bun.js/api.zig`

**Add** after line 34 (after `YAMLObject`):
```zig
pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");
```

### 6. `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig`

Three changes:

**a) In `BunObject` struct** (after line 70, after `YAML`):
```zig
pub const INI = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getINIObject);
```

**b) In `exportAll()`** (after line 140, after `YAML` export):
```zig
@export(&BunObject.INI, .{ .name = lazyPropertyCallbackName("INI") });
```

**c) Getter function** (after `getYAMLObject` at ~line 1289):
```zig
pub fn getINIObject(globalThis: *jsc.JSGlobalObject, _: *jsc.JSObject) jsc.JSValue {
    return INIObject.create(globalThis);
}
```

**d) Import at bottom** (after line 2083, after `YAMLObject`):
```zig
const INIObject = bun.api.INIObject;
```

### 7. `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h`

**Add** `macro(INI)` in the `FOR_EACH_GETTER` list (alphabetically between `Glob` and `JSON5`,
i.e., after line 11):
```c
macro(INI) \
```

### 8. `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`

**Add** in `@begin bunObjectTable` section (alphabetically near other format namespaces, 
after the `JSONL` line ~965):
```
INI                                            BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI                                       DontDelete|PropertyCallback
```

### 9. `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`

**Add** after the `JSONC` namespace (after line 821):
```ts
/**
 * INI related APIs
 */
namespace INI {
  /**
   * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.
   *
   * Supports sections (as nested objects), key=value pairs, and
   * comment lines starting with `;` or `#`. Inline comments are
   * also stripped from values.
   *
   * @category Utilities
   *
   * @param input The INI string to parse
   * @returns A JavaScript object
   *
   * @example
   * ```ts
   * import { INI } from "bun";
   *
   * const result = INI.parse(`
   *   ; comment
   *   name = Alice
   *
   *   [database]
   *   host = localhost
   *   port = 5432
   * `);
   * // { name: "Alice", database: { host: "localhost", port: "5432" } }
   * ```
   */
  export function parse(input: string): Record<string, string | Record<string, string>>;

  /**
   * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI string.
   *
   * Root-level scalar properties become flat `key = value` lines.
   * Root-level object properties become `[section]` blocks.
   * Arrays and values nested more than one level deep are not supported.
   *
   * @category Utilities
   *
   * @param input The JavaScript object to stringify
   * @returns A string containing the INI document
   *
   * @example
   * ```ts
   * import { INI } from "bun";
   *
   * const result = INI.stringify({
   *   name: "Alice",
   *   database: { host: "localhost", port: "5432" },
   * });
   * // name = Alice
   * // [database]
   * // host = localhost
   * // port = 5432
   * ```
   */
  export function stringify(input: Record<string, unknown>): string;
}
```

---

## Critical File Paths

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/interchange/ini.zig` | **CREATE** – pure Zig INI parser |
| `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` | **CREATE** – JS binding (parse + stringify) |
| `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts` | **CREATE** – test suite |
| `src/interchange.zig` | **MODIFY** – add `pub const ini` |
| `src/bun.js/api.zig` | **MODIFY** – add `pub const INIObject` |
| `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | **MODIFY** – 4 additions |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` | **MODIFY** – add `macro(INI)` |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` | **MODIFY** – add table entry |
| `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts` | **MODIFY** – add `namespace INI` |

---

## Existing Utilities to Reuse

| Utility | Location |
|---------|----------|
| `bun.ArenaAllocator` | Pattern from YAMLObject/JSON5Object |
| `ast.ASTMemoryAllocator` | Same arena setup as JSON5Object |
| `jsc.Node.BlobOrStringOrBuffer.fromJS` | Flexible input (strings, buffers) |
| `logger.Log` + `logger.Source.initPathString` | Consistent error logging |
| `exprToJS(Expr, global) JSValue` | Copy from JSON5Object.zig |
| `std.array_list.Managed(G.Property)` + `.moveFromList` | JSON5 parser AST building pattern |
| `jsc.JSPropertyIterator` | Used in YAMLObject stringify |
| `wtf.StringBuilder` + `.toString(global)` | Used in YAML/JSON5 stringify |
| `Expr.init(E.Object, .{ .properties = … }, loc)` | JSON5 parser pattern |
| `Expr.init(E.String, E.String{ .data = … }, loc)` | JSON5 parser pattern |

---

## Verification

After implementation (do NOT build during planning):

```bash
# Run the test file
bun bd test test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts

# Quick smoke test
bun bd -e "console.log(Bun.INI.parse('[s]\nk=v'))"
# Expected: { s: { k: 'v' } }

bun bd -e "console.log(Bun.INI.stringify({ name: 'Alice', db: { host: 'localhost' } }))"
# Expected: name = Alice\n[db]\nhost = localhost\n
```

Also verify TypeScript types are correct with:
```bash
cd packages/bun-types && bun run build 2>&1 | head -20
```

0	/// JSON5 Token-Based Scanner/Parser
1	///
2	/// Parses JSON5 text into Expr AST values. JSON5 is a superset of JSON
3	/// based on ECMAScript 5.1 that supports comments, trailing commas,
4	/// unquoted keys, single-quoted strings, hex numbers, Infinity, NaN, etc.
5	///
6	/// Architecture: a scanner reads source bytes and produces typed tokens;
7	/// the parser only consumes tokens and never touches source/pos directly.
8	///
9	/// Reference: https://spec.json5.org/
10	pub const JSON5Parser = struct {
11	    source: []const u8,
12	    pos: usize,
13	    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
14	    stack_check: bun.StackCheck,
15	    token: Token,
16	
17	    const Token = struct {
18	        loc: logger.Loc,
19	        data: Data,
20	
21	        const Data = union(enum) {
22	            eof,
23	            // Structural
24	            left_brace,
25	            right_brace,
26	            left_bracket,
27	            right_bracket,
28	            colon,
29	            comma,
30	            // Values
31	            string: []u8,
32	            number: f64,
33	            boolean: bool,
34	            null,
35	            // Identifiers (for unquoted keys that aren't keywords)
36	            identifier: []u8,
37	
38	            fn canStartValue(data: Data) bool {
39	                return switch (data) {
40	                    .string, .number, .boolean, .identifier, .null, .left_brace, .left_bracket => true,
41	                    .eof, .right_brace, .right_bracket, .colon, .comma => false,
42	                };
43	            }
44	        };
45	    };
46	
47	    const ParseError = OOM || error{
48	        UnexpectedCharacter,
49	        UnexpectedToken,
50	        UnexpectedEof,
51	        UnterminatedString,
52	        UnterminatedComment,
53	        UnterminatedObject,
54	        UnterminatedArray,
55	        UnterminatedEscape,
56	        InvalidNumber,
57	        LeadingZeros,
58	        InvalidHexNumber,
59	        InvalidHexEscape,
60	        InvalidUnicodeEscape,
61	        OctalEscape,
62	        ExpectedColon,
63	        ExpectedComma,
64	        ExpectedClosingBrace,
65	        ExpectedClosingBracket,
66	        InvalidIdentifier,
67	        TrailingData,
68	        StackOverflow,
69	    };
70	
71	    pub const Error = union(enum) {
72	        oom,
73	        stack_overflow,
74	        unexpected_character: struct { pos: usize },
75	        unexpected_token: struct { pos: usize },
76	        unexpected_eof: struct { pos: usize },
77	        unterminated_string: struct { pos: usize },
78	        unterminated_comment: struct { pos: usize },
79	        unterminated_object: struct { pos: usize },
80	        unterminated_array: struct { pos: usize },
81	        unterminated_escape: struct { pos: usize },
82	        invalid_number: struct { pos: usize },
83	        leading_zeros: struct { pos: usize },
84	        invalid_hex_number: struct { pos: usize },
85	        invalid_hex_escape: struct { pos: usize },
86	        invalid_unicode_escape: struct { pos: usize },
87	        octal_escape: struct { pos: usize },
88	        expected_colon: struct { pos: usize },
89	        expected_comma: struct { pos: usize },
90	        expected_closing_brace: struct { pos: usize },
91	        expected_closing_bracket: struct { pos: usize },
92	        invalid_identifier: struct { pos: usize },
93	        trailing_data: struct { pos: usize },
94	
95	        pub fn addToLog(this: *const Error, source: *const logger.Source, log: *logger.Log) (OOM || error{StackOverflow})!void {
96	            const loc: logger.Loc = switch (this.*) {
97	                .oom => return error.OutOfMemory,
98	                .stack_overflow => return error.StackOverflow,
99	                inline else => |e| .{ .start = @intCast(e.pos) },
100	            };
101	            const msg: []const u8 = switch (this.*) {
102	                .oom, .stack_overflow => unreachable,
103	                .unexpected_character => "Unexpected character",
104	                .unexpected_token => "Unexpected token",
105	                .unexpected_eof => "Unexpected end of input",
106	                .unterminated_string => "Unterminated string",
107	                .unterminated_comment => "Unterminated multi-line comment",
108	                .unterminated_object => "Unterminated object",
109	                .unterminated_array => "Unterminated array",
110	                .unterminated_escape => "Unexpected end of input in escape sequence",
111	                .invalid_number => "Invalid number",
112	                .leading_zeros => "Leading zeros are not allowed in JSON5",
113	                .invalid_hex_number => "Invalid hex number",
114	                .invalid_hex_escape => "Invalid hex escape",
115	                .invalid_unicode_escape => "Invalid unicode escape: expected 4 hex digits",
116	                .octal_escape => "Octal escape sequences are not allowed in JSON5",
117	                .expected_colon => "Expected ':' after object key",
118	                .expected_comma => "Expected ','",
119	                .expected_closing_brace => "Expected '}'",
120	                .expected_closing_bracket => "Expected ']'",
121	                .invalid_identifier => "Invalid identifier start character",
122	                .trailing_data => "Unexpected token after JSON5 value",
123	            };
124	            try log.addError(source, loc, msg);
125	        }
126	    };
127	
128	    fn toError(err: ParseError, parser: *const JSON5Parser) Error {
129	        const token_pos = parser.token.loc.toUsize();
130	        const scan_pos = parser.pos;
131	        return switch (err) {
132	            error.OutOfMemory => .oom,
133	            error.StackOverflow => .stack_overflow,
134	            // Scanner errors use scan position
135	            error.UnexpectedCharacter => .{ .unexpected_character = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
136	            error.UnterminatedString => .{ .unterminated_string = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
137	            error.UnterminatedComment => .{ .unterminated_comment = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
138	            error.UnterminatedEscape => .{ .unterminated_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
139	            error.InvalidNumber => .{ .invalid_number = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
140	            error.LeadingZeros => .{ .leading_zeros = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
141	            error.InvalidHexNumber => .{ .invalid_hex_number = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
142	            error.InvalidHexEscape => .{ .invalid_hex_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
143	            error.InvalidUnicodeEscape => .{ .invalid_unicode_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
144	            error.OctalEscape => .{ .octal_escape = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
145	            error.InvalidIdentifier => .{ .invalid_identifier = .{ .pos = scan_pos } },
146	            // Parser errors use token position
147	            error.UnexpectedToken => .{ .unexpected_token = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
148	            error.UnexpectedEof => if (parser.token.data == .eof)
149	                .{ .unexpected_eof = .{ .pos = token_pos } }
150	            else
151	                .{ .unexpected_token = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
152	            error.TrailingData => .{ .trailing_data = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
153	            error.ExpectedColon => .{ .expected_colon = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
154	            error.UnterminatedObject => .{ .unterminated_object = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
155	            error.ExpectedComma => .{ .expected_comma = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
156	            error.ExpectedClosingBrace => .{ .expected_closing_brace = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
157	            error.UnterminatedArray => .{ .unterminated_array = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
158	            error.ExpectedClosingBracket => .{ .expected_closing_bracket = .{ .pos = token_pos } },
159	        };
160	    }
161	
162	    const ExternalError = OOM || error{ SyntaxError, StackOverflow };
163	
164	    pub fn parse(source: *const logger.Source, log: *logger.Log, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ExternalError!Expr {
165	        var parser = JSON5Parser{
166	            .source = source.contents,
167	            .pos = 0,
168	            .allocator = allocator,
169	            .stack_check = .init(),
170	            .token = .{ .loc = .{}, .data = .eof },
171	        };
172	        const result = parser.parseRoot() catch |err| {
173	            const e = toError(err, &parser);
174	            try e.addToLog(source, log);
175	            return error.SyntaxError;
176	        };
177	        return result;
178	    }
179	
180	    // ── Scanner ──
181	
182	    /// Returns the byte at the current position, or 0 if at EOF.
183	    /// All source access in scan() goes through this to avoid bounds checks.
184	    fn peek(self: *const JSON5Parser) u8 {
185	        if (self.pos < self.source.len) return self.source[self.pos];
186	        return 0;
187	    }
188	
189	    fn scan(self: *JSON5Parser) ParseError!void {
190	        self.token.data = next: switch (self.peek()) {
191	            0 => {
192	                self.token.loc = .{ .start = @intCast(self.pos) };
193	                break :next .eof;
194	            },
195	            // Whitespace — skip without setting loc
196	            '\t', '\n', '\r', ' ', 0x0B, 0x0C => {
197	                self.pos += 1;
198	                continue :next self.peek();
199	            },

namespace YAML {
    /**
     * Parse a YAML string into a JavaScript value
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The YAML string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript value
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { YAML } from "bun";
     *
     * console.log(YAML.parse("123")) // 123
     * console.log(YAML.parse("null")) // null
     * console.log(YAML.parse("false")) // false
     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc")) // "abc"
     * console.log(YAML.parse("- abc")) // [ "abc" ]
     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc: def")) // { "abc": "def" }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): unknown;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript value into a YAML string. Strings are double quoted if they contain keywords, non-printable or
     * escaped characters, or if a YAML parser would parse them as numbers. Anchors and aliases are inferred from objects, allowing cycles.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify.
     * @param replacer Currently not supported.
     * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation.
     *              Without this parameter, outputs flow-style (single-line) YAML.
     *              With this parameter, outputs block-style (multi-line) YAML.
     *              The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used.
     * @returns A string containing the YAML document.
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { YAML } from "bun";
     *
     * const input = {
     *   abc: "def",
     *   num: 123
     * };
     *
     * // Without space - flow style (single-line)
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input));
     * // {abc: def,num: 123}
     *
     * // With space - block style (multi-line)
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input, null, 2));
     * // abc: def
     * // num: 123
     *
     * const cycle = {};
     * cycle.obj = cycle;
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(cycle, null, 2));
     * // &1
     * // obj: *1
     */
    export function stringify(input: unknown, replacer?: undefined | null, space?: string | number): string;
  }

  /**
   * Markdown related APIs.
   *
   * Provides fast markdown parsing and rendering with three output modes:
   * - `html()` — render to an HTML string
   * - `render()` — render with custom callbacks for each element

Now I have all the information I need. Let me implement the changes step by step:

1. Create `src/interchange/ini.zig` - The INI parser
2. Create `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` - The JS API binding
3. Create `test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts` - Test file
4. Modify `src/interchange.zig` - Add ini import
5. Modify `src/bun.js/api.zig` - Add INIObject import


6. Modify `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` - 4 additions
7. Modify `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` - Add INI macro
8. Modify `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` - Add table entry
9. Modify `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts` - Add INI namespace

Now I'm designing the INI parser to handle sections with bracket notation, key-value pairs using either equals or colon delimiters, and comment lines starting with semicolons or hashes.

For the implementation, I'll construct an `Expr` AST that represents the parsed INI as a nested object structure, where sections become nested objects and top-level keys stay at the root. The `stringify` function will reverse this by iterating through properties and emitting section headers for nested objects followed by their key-value pairs.

Looking at how the existing JSON5 parser constructs `Expr` nodes, I can see it uses variants like `e_null`, `e_boolean`, `e_number`, `e_string`, `e_array`, and `e_object` with properties to build the AST representation.

Since INI values are always strings with no type coercion, I could either build the `Expr` tree directly or construct the JS object more simply. Building the JS object directly seems cleaner and avoids unnecessary allocations, so I'll go with that approach for the parser implementation.

Writing the INI parser... Building the Expr AST structure...

I'm realizing I should follow the JSON5 parser's pattern more closely—using managed arrays of properties and the moveFromList approach to construct the nested object representation that the Expr type expects. I'm realizing that since INI is much simpler than JSON5—just flat key-value pairs within sections—I can build the JavaScript object directly rather than constructing an AST. This means the parse function would create an empty object, populate it while parsing the INI text, and return it directly. Given that the other parsers follow a pattern of having the real logic in a separate module, I should probably put the INI parsing machinery in `interchange/ini.zig` and have `INIObject.zig` call into it, keeping things consistent with the existing structure. I'm realizing that trying to build complex Expr AST values might be overcomplicating this—I should look at how existing parsers like json5 handle it, but for INI's simpler structure, the cleanest approach is probably to have `interchange/ini.zig` parse directly into a structured format that `INIObject.zig` can work with. I'm examining how the TOML parser constructs Expr values to understand the pattern I need to follow for the INI parser.

Checking the `ini.zig` parser logic for a potential issue: when `flushSection` gets called with no active section, it returns early, but I want to verify that `sec_props` can't accumulate orphaned properties. Tracing through the flow: we start with `active_section = null` and `sec_props` empty, flat keys go into `root_props` since there's no active section, and only when we encounter a section header do we set `active_section` and start populating `sec_props`.

When we hit the next section header, `flushSection` properly writes the accumulated properties for the current section and resets `sec_props`. This pattern continues through the end of input, so the logic checks out. Now I'm wondering about an edge case where the file doesn't end with a newline.

Looking at the line-parsing loop, if the input is just "key = value" without a trailing newline, the loop correctly processes it as a complete line since `nl` will be null, making `raw_line` the entire remaining string. After processing, `remaining` becomes empty and the loop exits cleanly.

For the section flushing logic, I'm concerned about whether `moveFromList` properly clears the original ArrayList's state before I reinitialize `sec_props`. The reinitialization should be safe, but I want to verify that the old list's backing array is actually transferred to the section object and not left in a dangling state. moveFromList` call, so even if `moveFromList` leaves it in an indeterminate state, my reset handles it.

Wait, actually `moveFromList` is called on `sec_props` inside `flushSection`:
```zig
.properties = .moveFromList(sec_props),
```

After this, `sec_props` is in a moved-from state (empty or undefined). Then I do:
```zig
sec_props.* = std.array_list.Managed(G.Property).init(allocator);
```

This explicitly reinitializes it. So it should be safe.

Now let me trace through the parsing flow more carefully. Starting with `active_section = null` and `sec_props` empty, flat keys go into `root_props` while section keys go into `sec_props`. When I encounter a new section header, `flushSection` gets called with the previous section name—if there was no previous section, it returns early without touching `sec_props`. Once I set a new `active_section`, subsequent keys append to `sec_props` until the next section header triggers another flush, which moves the accumulated properties and reinitializes the list. The logic holds up—when `active_section` is null, `sec_props` is always empty when `flushSection` gets called, so there's no risk of losing data. As for cleanup, calling `deinit()` on the moved-from list after `moveFromList` would be either harmless or problematic depending on the allocator state, but with an arena allocator it doesn't really matter since everything gets freed together anyway. All changes are in place. Here's a complete summary of everything that was implemented:

---

## Summary of changes

### New files (3)

**`src/interchange/ini.zig`** — Pure Zig INI parser that converts INI text into an `Expr` AST (`E.Object`). Handles:
- `[section_name]` headers → nested `E.Object` values
- `key = value` and `key: value` pairs (all values as `E.String`)
- Comment lines starting with `;` or `#` (skipped)
- Inline comments stripped after `;`/`#`, respecting single and double quotes
- CRLF and LF line endings, leading/trailing whitespace trimmed
- Malformed lines silently skipped

**`src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`** — JS API binding following the `JSON5Object.zig` pattern exactly:
- `create()` builds a JS object with `parse` and `stringify` methods
- `parse` accepts `string | Buffer | TypedArray`, calls `ini.parse()` via the arena + ASTMemoryAllocator pattern, then `exprToJS`
- `stringify` does two passes over the JS object with `JSPropertyIterator`: first emitting flat `key = value` lines, then `[section]` blocks for object values

**`test/js/bun/ini/ini.test.ts`** — Added two `describe` blocks for `Bun.INI.parse` and `Bun.INI.stringify` (34 tests) to the existing file, covering sections, comments, inline comment stripping, colon separators, Buffer/Uint8Array input, round-trip correctness, and edge cases.

### Modified files (6)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/interchange.zig` | `pub const ini = @import("./interchange/ini.zig");` |
| `src/bun.js/api.zig` | `pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");` |
| `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig` | Lazy property decl (`pub const INI`), `@export`, getter (`getINIObject`), and import (`const INIObject`) |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h` | `macro(INI)` added to `FOR_EACH_GETTER` (alphabetically between Glob and JSON5) |
| `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp` | `INI BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI DontDelete|PropertyCallback` hash table entry |
| `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts` | `namespace INI { parse(...): ...; stringify(...): string; }` with JSDoc |
